title: James Kelly
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: James Kelly
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

education_entries: Ph D in Mathematics, Baylor University
BS in Mathematics, Angelo State University
research_interests: Topology, continuum theory, and dynamical systems.
teaching_interests: I teach a wide variety of courses at all levels. I particularly enjoy teaching proof-based courses such as Proofs and Discrete Mathematics and Real Analysis.
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2021). Entropy conjugacy for Markov multi-maps of the interval . Discrete and Continuous Dynamical Systems. Volume, 41. Issue, 5. Pages, 2071-2094.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2021). Topological entropy of Markov set-valued functions . Ergodic Theory and Dynamical Systems. Volume, 41. Issue, 2. Pages, 321-337.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2020). Linear operators with infinite entropy . Journal of Mathematical Analysis and Applications. Volume, 487. Issue, 2.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2020). Direct Limits of Adele Rings and Their Completions . Rocky Mountain Journal of Mathematics. Volume, 50. Issue, 3. Pages, 1021-1043.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2019). Endpoints of inverse limits for a family of set-valued functions . Topology Proceedings. Volume, 54. Pages, 271-282.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2018). Markov set-vaued functions and their inverse limits . Topology and its Applications. Volume, 241. Pages, 101-114.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2018). Chaotic Dynamics in a Family of Set-Valued Functions . Minnesota Journal of Undergraduate Mathematics. Volume, 3. Pages, 18.

Grants and Sponsored Research: Neville Fogarty, Brian Bradie, James Kelly, Sara Lenhart, "A No-Cost, All-Inclusive Statistics Course," Sponsored by Virtual Library of Virginia
